key_points: Dorms reported as mold and bug-infested by multiple students
University Oaks has broken gates, flickering lights, dishwasher issues
Only community kitchens in Chap and Laurel dorms—no individual stoves
key_quotes: sleeping in mold and bug infested dorms... hope that slower internet was worth it
Does this factor in that UTSA students don't have individual stoves unless you live off campus or in the oaks?
Ive never seen gates broken... Maybe if you put a work order you would get your stuff fixed

key_points: Heavily commuter campus—most students drive in and leave quickly
Students describe peers as cold, passive-aggressive, hard to befriend
Organic campus community exists but requires effort to find it
key_quotes: Feels like everyone here hate talking to others except if its one of their friends. Passive aggressive even.
Alot of the people here are commuters and take like 60+ minutes to get to school... they just in bitchy moods tryna get home before work
all it takes is someone to piss all over campus again for people to come together and make friends

key_points: On-campus food options are decent but heavily overcrowded at peak times
Middle/high school tour groups flood food courts with zero warning to staff
New nearby HEB is a major win; free VIALink bus gets you there
key_quotes: I had an hour between classes and still didn't have time to get food because the lines were so packed with [tour kids]
a friend of mine works in the jpl food court and they get NO NOTICE when tours come in so all of a sudden they'll have a double rush
Subway, Chick fil a and Panda could never fail

key_points: Genuine peer support culture exists—students leave encouraging bathroom notes
Academic pressure and isolation are real, especially for commuters
No specific mental health resources called out; students lean on each other
key_quotes: I've been having such a terrible week and this note made me smile
I have no friends, despite being on my second year... I'm a depressed, anxious mess
please I beg of you, PLEASE call your loved ones... I promise you there is no bigger regret

key_points: Online orientation now costs $175—up from $50 in 2018, was once free
Mandatory add-on costs stack up: $175 orientation, $600-800/semester in platform fees
One Stop financial aid office notorious for 3+ hour hold times
key_quotes: Why is an online orientation $175??? It was like 80 bucks or something 2 years ago (still ridiculous)
if anyone can enlighten me on why McGraw Hill is optimal... i'm just tired of paying an extra $600-$800 per semester for some ebooks
Been on a '19 minute' hold for over 3 hours. Have yet to speak to a human.

key_points: Heavy-accent professors are a recurring, unresolved complaint in hard classes
A professor was caught charging students $115 for kits worth $20 via CashApp
McGraw Hill and Cengage platform fees are near-universal student grievances
key_quotes: they had an extremely heavy accent... they themselves couldn't say the words on the slides and would sometimes just mumble something
Robert Lucio was the professors name and students were paying to his cashapp. Pretty sure UTSA is just sweeping it under the rug
there's no way broke college students are expected to pay almost 200 for a 4 month online textbook in 2026

key_points: University diverts 32% of Rec Center student fees to unspecified uses
Pay study for staff employees stalled for 10+ years while new facilities get built
Graduation ceremony is disorganized and overcrowded—families publicly disappointed
key_quotes: The higher ups in the University have been taking about 32% of that money and putting it elsewhere
They were still telling that pay study lie when I was there 10 years ago. I got sick of 7 years without even a cost of living raise
my whole family was extremely disappointed with how the ceremony went... it is clear UTSA doesn't care about their students they just want to save $

key_points: Parking is a campus-wide crisis—illegal spots, two-spot parkers, even cars driving through courtyards
No safe walkable path to the new nearby HEB until firefighters cleared a trail
Crosswalks are dangerously faded; drivers routinely ignore pedestrian right-of-way
key_quotes: I deadass just stood here for 2 minutes waiting for a car to actually let me walk across
I tried walking over to HEB yesterday from Chisholm but gave up because I didn't want to go through the trees
the newly painted crosswalks on barshop suck too. the stripes have no grip when wet. ive nearly eaten shit on them

key_points: Cybersecurity program has national reputation and produces employable graduates
San Antonio's limited tech industry is a real concern for CS/tech career seekers
Perceived as a tier-2 school by many Texas students; affects employer perception
key_quotes: San Antonio doesn't have what most would consider prestigious universities... UTSA which I think is like a tier 2 college
I am very skeptical that any FAANG company is going to come to San Antonio to recruit
UTSA is not highly ranked/revered by any means. However, while staying in San Antonio I am able to live with family and save

key_points: Low tuition and living-at-home options make it a genuine value for locals
Fee creep is real: orientation, textbook platforms, parking, Rec fees all bite
Student loan payoff posts show 19-year debt terms are a real outcome here
key_quotes: $15k tuition btw... [and] we have to pay $28 million for a mediocre coach
i pay so much for tuition and they can't pay an artist to paint a mural
Took 19 years but totally worth it [paying off student loan]

key_points: Campus has genuine quirky lore and humor—the Grey Mustang meme unites students
Strong first-gen, Hispanic-serving identity creates real community for many
Feels like extended high school to some; others find warmth and belonging
key_quotes: Congrats on your admission to Whataburger University lol. In all seriousness though congrats
You'll be complaining about gray mustangs in no time
Dude this place is literally extended High School to some people

key_points: **Safety:** Attempted sexual assault reported on trail near campus in 2026
**Administration:** Rec Center fees skimmed, pay study suppressed, AI art used instead of student artists
**Academics:** Professor ran CashApp scam on students; UTSA response was silence
key_quotes: a female victim while the suspect attempted to sexually assault her at the Leon Creek Greenway Park, near UTSA
Pretty sure UTSA is just sweeping it under the rug since he was probably making thousands of dollars from college students
Some art major is slowly realizing rn that maybe the college teaching them doesnt even have faith in their degree

key_points: Student-built RateUTSA tool combines RateMyProfessor and Bluebook data in one place
Free VIALink rideshare for all students—functions as a free on-campus shuttle
Campus wildlife, secret study spots, and genuine student kindness create unexpected warmth
key_quotes: rateutsa combines data from both bluebook and ratemyprofessors so you can see everything in one place
you get a Upass for free as a UTSA student... you can technically use VIALink as a shuttle
I remember years ago one had died near the upper floor courtyard window... over several days little notes and poems and drawings from random students began to appear
